metadata.name: Rubber
metadata.title: Rubber Laser Cleaning Settings
metadata.description: Detailed machine settings, parameter relationships, diagnostic procedures, and troubleshooting guides for optimizing rubber laser cleaning operations.

author.name: Todd Dunning
author.title: MA
credentials: MA Optics and Photonics, UC Irvine, 2019
author.imageAlt: Todd Dunning, MA, Junior Optical Materials Specialist at Coherent Inc., with precision optics tools

powerRange.description: Optimal average power for rubber cleaning without thermal damage

wavelength.description: Near-infrared wavelength for optimal rubber absorption and minimal substrate damage

spotSize.description: Beam spot diameter for precise rubber removal with adequate cleaning area

repetitionRate.description: Optimal repetition rate for efficient rubber cleaning with good process control

energyDensity.description: Fluence threshold for effective rubber ablation while preserving substrate integrity

pulseWidth.description: Nanosecond pulse duration for effective rubber ablation with controlled thermal effects

scanSpeed.description: Optimal scanning speed for uniform rubber removal without excessive heat accumulation

passCount.description: Recommended number of passes for complete rubber removal with process control

overlapRatio.description: Optimal beam overlap for uniform rubber removal without excessive processing time
